prompts.title: Draft Press Release for Indian Product Launch
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PR consultant with 12 years of experience at Adfactors PR handling consumer tech launches in India. I need you to draft a 450-word press release announcing the launch of a new D2C electric scooter (priced INR 1.29 lakh ex-showroom Mumbai) by a Bengaluru-based startup, Voltify Mobility. Here is the context: launch event at Taj Lands End on May 14, 2026, with the founder (IIT-Madras alum, ex-Ola) and chief guest Union Minister Nitin Gadkari (TBC). The product offers 165 km range, FAME-II subsidy eligibility, and bookings open at INR 999. Please provide: (1) a tight headline under 12 words and a sub-head under 18 words, (2) Mumbai-datelined lead paragraph with the 5 Ws, (3) three body paragraphs covering specs, pricing/subsidy math in INR, and CEO quote, (4) one investor/dealer quote, (5) standard "About Voltify" boilerplate (75 words), (6) media contact block with name, mobile (+91 format), and email. Format as a copy-paste-ready release with proper PR formatting (FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE header, ###, -ENDS-). Constraints: avoid superlatives banned by ASCI, no comparative claims against Ola/Ather/TVS without data citation, ensure FAME-II references are accurate, keep tone factual yet aspirational suitable for ET Auto, Autocar India, and Mint reporters.

prompts.title: Write Media Pitch Email to ET Tech Reporter
prompts.prompt: Act as an account director at Edelman India with 9 years of experience pitching B2B SaaS stories to Indian business press. I need you to write a 220-word media pitch email to a senior tech reporter at The Economic Times (Bengaluru bureau) covering enterprise SaaS. Here is the context: my client is Razorpay-backed fintech "Finlayer", which has just crossed INR 500 crore ARR, signed HDFC Bank as a customer, and raised a USD 80 million Series D led by Tiger Global at a USD 1.4 billion valuation (Indian unicorn #115). Embargo lifts April 30, 2026, 10:00 AM IST. Please provide: (1) a personalised subject line under 60 characters referencing the reporter's recent SaaS coverage, (2) a 3-line opener that hooks with the unicorn angle and HDFC marquee win, (3) middle section with three crisp bullets (ARR milestone, funding details, India SaaS market context), (4) an exclusive offer (founder 1:1 + financial deck under embargo), (5) clear CTA proposing a 20-minute call slot, (6) signature with mobile and Calendly link. Constraints: avoid jargon like "revolutionary", do not over-promise exclusivity if pitched elsewhere, ensure embargo language is unambiguous, follow ET's known preference for hard numbers over adjectives. Format as a ready-to-send email with subject line on top.

prompts.title: Crisis Communication Statement for Bank Data Breach
prompts.prompt: Act as a crisis communications lead at Avian WE with 14 years of BFSI experience handling RBI-regulated entities. I need you to draft a 320-word crisis statement for a mid-sized private bank ("Meridian Bank", 480 branches, headquartered in Mumbai) following a data breach exposing 2.3 lakh customer records (names, mobile numbers, masked account numbers - no PINs/CVVs). Breach disclosed by CERT-In on April 26, 2026; bank has 6 hours before TV news cycle peaks. Please provide: (1) holding statement under 80 words for first response within 60 minutes, (2) detailed statement covering acknowledgement, scope, customer impact, remedial steps (forensic audit by EY, RBI/CERT-In notification, free credit monitoring for affected customers), and CEO accountability quote, (3) FAQ block of 6 likely customer questions with approved answers, (4) social media post versions for X/Twitter (under 280 chars) and LinkedIn, (5) internal employee note from MD explaining talking points. Constraints: comply with RBI Master Direction on Cyber Security, DPDP Act 2023 notification timelines, avoid speculative attribution to threat actors, do not minimise impact, no "unprecedented" framing. Tone must balance contrition with reassurance. Format as five clearly labelled sections ready for legal sign-off.

prompts.title: Event Coverage Brief for Mumbai Press Conference
prompts.prompt: Act as a PR manager at Madison PR with 7 years of experience executing press conferences for Indian conglomerates. I need you to prepare a comprehensive event coverage brief for a press conference where Aditya Birla Group announces a INR 2,400 crore investment in a new specialty chemicals plant in Bharuch, Gujarat. Event: May 8, 2026, 11:00 AM at Trident BKC, Mumbai. Please provide: (1) event run-of-show with 15-minute time blocks from 09:30 registration to 13:30 wrap, (2) target media list of 35 outlets segmented into business dailies (Mint, ET, BS, BL), wires (PTI, ANI, Reuters, Bloomberg), trade press (Chemical Weekly, Indian Chemical News), and broadcast (ET Now, CNBC TV18, BTVi), (3) reporter beat-mapping with 8 named priority journalists and their recent coverage angles, (4) collateral checklist (press kit USB, printed factsheet, B-roll, photo ops), (5) Q&A anticipation matrix with 10 tough questions on capex funding, China+1 dependency, and environmental clearances, (6) post-event distribution timeline for release, photos, and video. Format as a structured brief with tables. Constraints: factor Mumbai monsoon risk for May, ensure GIDC and MoEF references are accurate, plan for one regional Gujarati outlet (Divya Bhaskar).

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep Doc for CNBC TV18 Interview
prompts.prompt: Act as a media training consultant with 11 years of experience prepping Indian C-suite executives for business news appearances. I need you to build a spokesperson prep document for the CFO of an Indian IT services company ("Tatva Technologies", USD 4.2 billion revenue, listed on NSE) ahead of a 12-minute live interview on CNBC TV18 with Latha Venkatesh on Q4 FY26 results day. Context: revenue grew 7.8% YoY in constant currency, EBIT margin compressed 80 bps to 22.4%, deal TCV at USD 1.1 billion, BFSI vertical weak, GenAI revenue contribution disclosed for first time at 4.2%. Please provide: (1) 5 core messages in priority order with supporting data points, (2) bridging phrases to redirect from tough questions, (3) anticipated 12-question grill including margin guidance, attrition (now 12.8%), US visa cost impact, and Infosys/TCS comparisons, (4) sample answers in 30-45 second TV-friendly soundbites, (5) what NOT to say list (forward-looking statements, peer commentary, deal-specific names), (6) wardrobe and body-language notes for studio. Constraints: comply with SEBI LODR Regulation 30 disclosure norms, avoid US GAAP commentary that triggers Reg FD risk, factor Latha's known follow-up style. Format as a printable 4-page brief.

prompts.title: Press Release for Bollywood Brand Endorsement
prompts.prompt: Act as an entertainment PR specialist at Hype PR with 8 years of experience handling celebrity endorsements in India. I need you to draft a 400-word press release announcing actress Alia Bhatt as the new global brand ambassador for a homegrown jewellery brand "Zaveri Heritage" (Surat-based, 220 stores across India). Two-year deal valued at INR 24 crore. Campaign launch coincides with Akshaya Tritiya 2026 (May 9). Please provide: (1) headline and Mumbai-dateline lead capturing the festive marketing angle, (2) two paragraphs on brand strategy, ambassador rationale, and target Tier-2/3 expansion plan, (3) one quote each from the brand's MD and Alia Bhatt (in her voice, warm and rooted), (4) campaign details (TVC director, AOR "Wieden+Kennedy India", media spend INR 60 crore over 18 months), (5) About-the-brand boilerplate referencing 1978 founding in Surat, (6) media contact block. Constraints: ensure ASCI Celebrity Endorsement Guidelines compliance language, avoid references to competitor Tanishq/Kalyan, no health/wellness claims, keep tone aspirational but culturally rooted. Format as a publication-ready release suitable for Pinkvilla, exchange4media, Campaign India, Bombay Times, and Femina.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Cricket Sponsorship Story
prompts.prompt: Act as a sports PR strategist at Concept PR with 10 years of experience pitching cricket commercial stories. I need you to write a media pitch email to the sports business reporter at Mint and a parallel version for Cricbuzz. Client context: a fintech app "PaiseUp" has just signed a three-year, INR 75 crore associate sponsorship with Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) starting IPL 2027, replacing an existing apparel partner. Announcement embargoed until May 2, 2026. Please provide: (1) two distinct subject lines tailored to business angle (Mint) versus fan angle (Cricbuzz), (2) Mint version (200 words) emphasising deal economics, IPL sponsorship inflation, fintech category share, and CMO quote, (3) Cricbuzz version (180 words) emphasising fan engagement, Virat Kohli activations, and matchday integrations, (4) attached assets list (jersey mock, MOU snippet, founder bio), (5) follow-up plan with three touchpoints over 5 days, (6) talking points if either outlet pushes for exclusivity. Constraints: respect BCCI commercial guidelines, do not disclose specific player image rights numbers, avoid IPL trademark misuse, keep RBI ad-tech compliance in mind for fintech. Format as two ready-to-send emails plus a one-page strategy note.

prompts.title: Crisis Statement for Pharma Drug Recall
prompts.prompt: Act as a healthcare PR director at Genesis BCW with 15 years of experience handling Indian pharma crises. I need you to draft a crisis communication package for a top-10 Indian pharma company ("Saraswati Pharma", listed on BSE, USD 2.1 billion revenue) executing a voluntary recall of 14 batches of a hypertension drug (Telmisartan 40mg) due to nitrosamine impurity exceeding USFDA limits, also distributed in India. Trigger: USFDA Form 483 observation on April 22, 2026; Indian CDSCO notified. Please provide: (1) initial 90-word holding statement for the BSE filing under SEBI LODR Reg 30, (2) detailed 350-word public statement for media, (3) doctor-and-chemist letter (220 words) with batch numbers and replacement protocol, (4) patient FAQ in Hindi-friendly plain English (8 Q&A), (5) social listening triggers and response trees for Twitter/X complaints, (6) IVR script for the toll-free helpline. Constraints: align with CDSCO, USFDA, and Schedule M references, avoid admission of fault language pre-investigation, factor patient safety as paramount tone, avoid stock-price-sensitive forward statements. Format as six labelled sections with version control header.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Bengaluru Tech Summit
prompts.prompt: Act as a tech-PR account lead at Weber Shandwick India with 8 years of experience producing thought-leadership events. I need you to build an event coverage brief for "FutureStack India 2026", a one-day enterprise AI summit at The Leela Palace Bengaluru on May 22, 2026, hosted by my client (a US enterprise AI company entering India). Expected: 350 CIOs, 12 sessions, NASSCOM tie-up. Please provide: (1) tiered media invite list (Tier-1 ET, Mint, BS; Tier-2 YourStory, Inc42, Moneycontrol; Tier-3 Analytics India Magazine, ETCIO), (2) speaker-press matchmaking grid pairing 6 keynote speakers with relevant journalists, (3) session-wise news hook map identifying 4 announcement moments, (4) on-ground PR ops plan (registration, media lounge, 1:1 interview rooms, B-roll capture), (5) post-event content plan with by-line articles, video snippets, and trade-press follow-ups (Pitch, exchange4media, afaqs), (6) success metrics dashboard (AVE, share-of-voice vs. AWS Summit India, sentiment). Constraints: factor Bengaluru traffic for AM travel, NASSCOM co-branding rules, GST invoicing for media transport. Format as a 6-section brief with timelines and owner columns.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for AajTak Hindi News Hour
prompts.prompt: Act as a Hindi-language media coach with 13 years of experience prepping public-sector and consumer-brand spokespeople for Hindi prime-time television. I need you to prepare a spokesperson briefing for the MD of a state-run insurance major ("Bharat Jeevan Bima") for an 18-minute appearance on AajTak's prime-time news hour, where the topic is rising health insurance premiums (up 18% YoY) and customer grievances post IRDAI's new regulations. Please provide: (1) 4 core messages in chaste Hindi with English equivalents, (2) the spokesperson's three personal anecdotes/data points to humanise responses, (3) bridging phrases in Hindi ("Aapka sawaal sahi hai, lekin asal mudda yeh hai..."), (4) anticipated 10 tough questions from anchor including premium hikes, claim ratios (87.4%), and Ayushman Bharat overlap, (5) Hindi-friendly soundbites of 20-30 seconds for digital clips, (6) red-flag topics to avoid (PSU vs. private comparisons, agent commissions). Constraints: comply with IRDAI advertising regulations, avoid policy commitments not yet board-approved, respect AajTak's interruptive interview style. Format as a bilingual prep doc with Devanagari and Roman script.

prompts.title: Press Release for IPO Filing with SEBI
prompts.prompt: Act as a capital markets PR specialist at Adfactors PR with 10 years of experience handling Indian IPO communications. I need you to draft a press release announcing the filing of DRHP with SEBI by an Indian D2C beauty brand ("GlowKart", Pune-based, INR 1,180 crore FY26 revenue, INR 142 crore PAT). Issue size INR 3,500 crore (fresh issue INR 1,500 cr + OFS INR 2,000 cr). Lead bankers: Kotak, ICICI Securities, Axis Capital, JM Financial. Please provide: (1) compliant headline avoiding any "forward-looking" language, (2) Mumbai-dated release lead, (3) three body paragraphs covering business overview, financials snapshot in INR crore, and use-of-proceeds, (4) standard SEBI disclaimer block (verbatim mandatory text), (5) About-the-company section emphasising track record, (6) media-contact block. Constraints: strictly comply with SEBI ICDR Regulations 2018 publicity restrictions during the offer period, no projections, no comparisons with Mamaearth/Nykaa, no quotes from promoters that go beyond DRHP language, mandatory DRHP-link disclosure. Format as a SEBI-compliant ready-to-issue release with a separate "What we cannot say" annexure for the founder team.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Indian Startup Funding Round
prompts.prompt: Act as a startup PR consultant with 6 years of experience pitching Series A-C funding stories to Indian tech press. I need you to write a coordinated pitch email set for a Bengaluru-based climate-tech startup "GreenGrid" announcing a USD 28 million Series B led by Sequoia India (Peak XV Partners) with participation from Aavishkaar Capital. Embargo: May 5, 2026, 09:00 IST. Please provide: (1) master pitch (180 words) tailored for YourStory and Inc42 with emphasis on founder journey, (2) variant for ET (170 words) emphasising deal value, sector inflow trends, and INR-USD context, (3) variant for Moneycontrol (160 words) emphasising public-market read-through, (4) target list of 14 reporters with personalisation hooks based on their last 3 articles, (5) follow-up cadence over 7 days, (6) suggested exclusive offer to one outlet with risk assessment. Constraints: avoid "unicorn" claims unless valuation crosses USD 1 billion, ensure consistency across versions, respect investor PR clearance from Peak XV's comms team. Format as four ready-to-send emails plus a coordination tracker.

prompts.title: Crisis Comms Plan for IT Services Layoffs
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior crisis comms partner at Edelman India with 17 years of experience handling Indian IT services reputation issues. I need you to design a 5-day crisis communication plan for a top-5 Indian IT services major ("Suryadaya Infotech", 320,000 employees) that has decided to lay off 4,800 mid-senior employees citing automation and reduced US discretionary spend. News leaked on Glassdoor and Reddit on April 25, 2026. Please provide: (1) Day-0 to Day-5 communication calendar with internal and external touchpoints, (2) CEO town-hall script (8 minutes), (3) external statement for media (180 words) calibrated for ET, BS, Moneycontrol, (4) talking points for 1:1 reporter conversations with NASSCOM context, (5) employee FAQ covering severance (3 months base + INR 50,000 outplacement allowance), notice period, and ESOP vesting, (6) social-listening + dark-site readiness checklist. Constraints: comply with Industrial Disputes Act notifications, SEBI LODR for material event disclosure, factor union-led TN/Karnataka labour department interest. Format as a 5-day Gantt-style plan with owners and approvals matrix.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Delhi Auto Expo Stand
prompts.prompt: Act as an automotive PR account director at Madison PR with 9 years of experience handling Auto Expo coverage. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for an Indian commercial vehicle major ("Bharat Trucks", Pune-based, market cap INR 78,000 crore) for their pavilion at Auto Expo 2027, Bharat Mandapam, Delhi (Jan 10-15, 2027). Stand size 2,400 sqm, four product unveils. Please provide: (1) media-day run-of-show for Day 1 (07:00 to 16:00) with 8 timed unveil moments, (2) press conference structure with seating chart for 180 journalists, (3) 1:1 interview slot grid for CEO/CTO across 12 priority publications (Autocar Pro, ET Auto, Mint, BS, Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, ET Now, Bombay Times, Hindustan Times, Pitch, Campaign), (4) embargo strategy for the flagship hydrogen truck, (5) media-kit contents (USB, printed brochure, photos, MSME-supplier story angles), (6) post-show distribution to Tier-2/3 trade press (Commercial Vehicle, MotorIndia). Constraints: factor Delhi winter cold for outdoor moments, ARAI homologation language accuracy, avoid Tata/Ashok Leyland direct comparisons. Format as a 2-page brief with a one-page contingency tree.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for ET Now Markets Show
prompts.prompt: Act as a financial-markets media coach with 12 years of experience prepping fund managers and CEOs for Indian business television. I need you to prepare a briefing document for the CEO of a listed mid-cap NBFC ("Vridhi Finserv", AUM INR 32,000 crore, NSE-listed) ahead of an 8-minute interview on ET Now post Q4 results. Stock corrected 9% in pre-market on lower NIM (4.6% vs. 5.2% YoY) and rising GNPA (3.1%). Please provide: (1) opening 30-second statement framing the quarter positively, (2) 4 key messages with INR-denominated proof points, (3) bridging language to redirect from GNPA without dismissing it, (4) anticipated 9 questions including RBI's draft project finance guidelines, ALM concerns, and peer Bajaj Finance comparisons, (5) sample TV-ready answers under 35 seconds each, (6) red-line topics requiring "will get back" responses. Constraints: SEBI Reg 30 / FUTP regulations on price-sensitive information, avoid forward-looking guidance not in investor presentation, ASCI rules for financial-services communication. Format as a 3-page brief plus one-page "flashcard" for the green-room.

prompts.title: Press Release for FMCG Rural Expansion
prompts.prompt: Act as an FMCG PR account manager at Concept PR with 7 years of experience handling consumer brands. I need you to draft a 380-word press release for an Indian heritage FMCG brand ("Annapurna Foods", Kolkata-based, INR 4,200 crore revenue) announcing a INR 320 crore investment to add 18,000 rural distributors across UP, Bihar, MP, and Odisha over 24 months under "Mission Bharat 25". Please provide: (1) headline focused on rural reach and Atmanirbhar Bharat alignment, (2) Kolkata-dateline lead, (3) three body paragraphs on distribution model, women-entrepreneur (20% target) component, and SKU strategy (sachets at INR 1, INR 5, INR 10), (4) one quote from MD and one from VP Sales, (5) state-wise rural penetration data table, (6) About-the-company boilerplate emphasising 1956 heritage. Constraints: avoid claims around competing with HUL/Nestle/Britannia, FSSAI references must be accurate, ASCI compliance for affordability claims, sensitivity to MSP/agriculture-linked rural narrative. Format as a release suited to Mint, Business Standard, ETRetail, ET FMCG, and regional outlets like Anandabazar Patrika and Dainik Jagran.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Indian Fashion Week Show
prompts.prompt: Act as a lifestyle PR specialist with 8 years of experience handling fashion and luxury accounts in India. I need you to write a 200-word media pitch email to the senior fashion editor at Vogue India and an alternate version for The Hindu's Lifestyle desk. Context: my client is designer Tarun Tahiliani's couture house presenting a 42-piece occasion-wear collection at Lakme Fashion Week x FDCI on August 14, 2026, at JIO World Convention Centre, Mumbai. Showstopper: actress Deepika Padukone in a hand-embroidered lehenga. Please provide: (1) Vogue India version with luxury-fashion-vocabulary subject line and emphasis on craft/atelier story, (2) The Hindu version emphasising karigar livelihoods (340 artisans in Lucknow and Bareilly), (3) bespoke front-row invite language for both editors, (4) exclusive offer (atelier visit + designer 1:1) without overlap, (5) image and look-book delivery plan via WeTransfer, (6) three follow-up touchpoints. Constraints: respect FDCI/LFW media accreditation rules, avoid celebrity contract leaks, Vogue's preference for original imagery, The Hindu's aversion to overt brand-pushing. Format as two send-ready emails.

prompts.title: Crisis Statement for Restaurant Food Safety Issue
prompts.prompt: Act as a hospitality PR consultant with 10 years of experience handling F&B crises in India. I need you to draft a crisis communication kit for a 64-outlet QSR chain ("Spice Junction", HQ Gurugram) after FSSAI Bengaluru flagged contamination at one Koramangala outlet leading to 11 hospitalisations on April 24, 2026. Outlet has been suspended; story is trending on Reddit India and Twitter/X with #SpiceJunctionFood. Please provide: (1) 75-word holding statement for first hour, (2) detailed 280-word public statement, (3) social-media micro-copy for X/Twitter (3 versions), Instagram Story, and LinkedIn, (4) WhatsApp / email apology to all 1.4 lakh active loyalty members with INR 500 voucher offer, (5) franchisee internal note covering audit and SOP refresh, (6) talking points for the founder for a single TV interview (NDTV Food). Constraints: align with FSSAI Section 31 obligations, do not blame franchisee publicly, avoid blanket health-safety guarantees, factor IT Rules 2021 for influencer responses. Format as six labelled sections with timestamps for each release.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Hyderabad Pharma Conference
prompts.prompt: Act as a healthcare-PR project lead at Avian WE with 9 years of experience handling pharma conferences. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for "BioPharma Bharat 2026", a two-day conference at HICC Novotel, Hyderabad on June 18-19, 2026, hosted by my client (an Indian biotech firm "Nakshatra Biosciences") in association with FICCI. Expected: 280 delegates, 4 product announcements, Telangana IT Minister attending. Please provide: (1) two-day media run-of-show with key announcement moments, (2) media list of 32 outlets across pharma trade (Pharmabiz, Express Pharma, BioSpectrum), business press (ET Health, Mint, BS), and regional (Sakshi, Eenadu, Telangana Today), (3) speaker-journalist matchmaking grid for 8 keynote sessions, (4) Q&A anticipation matrix on regulatory, pricing (NPPA), and patent issues, (5) media-room logistics (Wi-Fi 200 Mbps, printers, charging, biryani lunch), (6) post-event content distribution plan including a CEO byline for ET Health. Constraints: NPPA price-control sensitivity, DCGI references must be accurate, factor Hyderabad airport drive-time. Format as a structured 2-day brief with checklists.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for Lok Sabha TV Panel
prompts.prompt: Act as a public-affairs media coach with 14 years of experience prepping industry-association leaders for parliamentary and policy television. I need you to prepare a panel-discussion brief for the Director General of an Indian industry body ("Bharat Manufacturing Council") for a 45-minute Sansad TV panel on the upcoming PLI 2.0 scheme expansion (April 30, 2026). Other panelists: NITI Aayog member, opposition MP, an academic. Please provide: (1) 5 priority talking points aligning industry asks with national interest, (2) 3 supportive data points (manufacturing GVA, employment elasticity, SME participation), (3) bridging phrases to deflect partisan attacks, (4) anticipated 12 questions across PLI utilisation, MSME inclusion, and China dependency, (5) sample 40-second answers calibrated for Lok Sabha-TV audience, (6) wardrobe and demeanour notes (no logos, sober palette). Constraints: maintain non-partisan tone per industry-body charter, avoid criticising specific ministries, factor Election Code if any state polls underway. Format as a 4-page brief plus a single-page flashcard.

prompts.title: Press Release for CSR Initiative Launch
prompts.prompt: Act as a corporate-comms manager at Genesis BCW with 6 years of experience handling CSR storytelling for Indian conglomerates. I need you to draft a 360-word press release announcing a INR 80 crore, three-year CSR initiative ("Padho Bharat") by an Indian cement major ("Hindusthan Cements", Mumbai-listed) to digitise 1,200 government primary schools across Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, and Jharkhand in partnership with Pratham Education Foundation. Please provide: (1) headline focused on schools and student impact (not the company), (2) Mumbai-dateline lead with INR commitment and beneficiary numbers, (3) three body paragraphs on programme design (smart classrooms, teacher training, learning outcomes measurement), (4) one quote each from CMD and Pratham CEO Rukmini Banerji, (5) state-wise breakdown table, (6) About-the-company and About-Pratham boilerplates. Constraints: comply with Companies Act Section 135 CSR rules and disclosure norms, avoid linking CSR to commercial benefits, NCERT and NEP 2020 references must be accurate, sensitivity to government school positioning. Format as a publication-ready release suitable for Mint, ET, BS, EducationWorld, and regional papers.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Banking Survey Findings
prompts.prompt: Act as a financial-services PR specialist with 9 years of experience pitching research-led stories to Indian business press. I need you to write a 220-word media pitch email to senior banking reporters at Mint, Business Standard, and Moneycontrol. Context: my client (a Big-4 consulting firm) has authored an India banking outlook report sampling 1,800 customers and 24 banks, finding (a) 62% of millennials prefer fintech apps over bank apps, (b) PSU bank NPS gap at -18 vs. private at +24, (c) GenAI deployment in banks projected to save INR 18,500 crore annually by FY28. Embargo: May 6, 2026, 11:00 AM. Please provide: (1) three subject-line variants A/B-test ready, (2) email body with hook, three killer data points, and CTA, (3) tailored exclusive angle for each of the three outlets, (4) press-kit attachment list (executive summary PDF, infographics, author bios), (5) follow-up cadence (T+1, T+3, T+5 days), (6) interview-availability grid for two report authors. Constraints: respect RBI's discomfort with bank-name-shaming, attribute sample data carefully, avoid speculative regulatory commentary. Format as a ready-to-send email with three personalised variants.

prompts.title: Crisis Statement for Aviation Incident
prompts.prompt: Act as an aviation-PR specialist with 13 years of experience handling Indian airline crisis comms. I need you to draft a crisis communication response for a domestic carrier ("SkyAir", 110 aircraft, fourth-largest by passenger share) following a tail-strike incident during landing at Bengaluru airport on April 27, 2026. No injuries among the 184 passengers; aircraft grounded for inspection by DGCA. Trending #SkyAirScare on X. Please provide: (1) 60-word holding tweet within 20 minutes, (2) 90-word press statement within 90 minutes, (3) detailed 300-word follow-up statement after preliminary findings (4 hours later), (4) passenger-care script for ground staff and call centre, (5) talking points for CEO interview with NDTV Profit and Aaj Tak, (6) social-listening response tree for misinformation. Constraints: comply with DGCA Section 8 reporting timelines, avoid causation statements pre-investigation, never speculate on pilot/maintenance, BCAS coordination, factor passengers' relatives' anxiety. Format as time-stamped sections with named approvers (Ops, Legal, CEO).

prompts.title: Event Brief for Mumbai Awards Ceremony
prompts.prompt: Act as an awards-show PR producer with 7 years of experience handling industry awards in India. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for the "India Marketing Excellence Awards 2026" hosted by my client (a media network) at Jio World Centre, Mumbai on June 11, 2026. 32 award categories, 600 attendees, host comedian Vir Das. Please provide: (1) two-hour media run-of-show including red-carpet, ceremony, and after-party windows, (2) media list of 28 trade publications (exchange4media, Pitch, Campaign India, afaqs, Brand Equity, Storyboard) and 10 mainstream outlets, (3) photographer and videographer rights brief with 6 official partners, (4) winner-press matchmaking for top-5 awards (Brand of the Year, CMO of the Year, etc.), (5) post-event content kit (winner reels, judge interviews, by-line for Pitch), (6) sentiment-monitoring plan for the 72 hours after. Constraints: factor Mumbai monsoon backup, alcohol-service compliance for Mumbai venues, avoid leaking winners pre-event, sponsor logo placement rules. Format as a structured brief with a separate red-carpet runsheet.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for Mint Print Interview
prompts.prompt: Act as a print-media coach with 10 years of experience prepping CEOs for long-form business interviews in India. I need you to prepare an interview brief for the founder-CEO of a Bengaluru SaaS company ("Karya Systems", USD 220 million ARR) ahead of a 90-minute interview with Mint's Editor-in-Chief covering the planned US listing route. Please provide: (1) 5 strategic narrative anchors for the interview, (2) 4 personal-story arcs to humanise the founder (early days at IIIT Hyderabad, first customer, near-bankruptcy in 2018), (3) 12 anticipated questions including Indian DPIIT reverse-flip rules, NSE/BSE versus Nasdaq listing economics, GenAI impact on SaaS pricing, (4) approved-answer outlines (not scripts) with 3 quotable phrases per topic, (5) deflection language for product-roadmap and competitor questions, (6) photo-shoot attire and location guidance for Mint's signature interview portraits. Constraints: respect SEBI/SEC pre-listing publicity restrictions, no specific revenue-guidance numbers, careful framing of employee count in India vs. US. Format as a 5-page brief with quotable-quotes annexure.

prompts.title: Press Release for NSE Listing Day Bell Ringing
prompts.prompt: Act as a capital-markets PR account director at Adfactors PR with 12 years of IPO listing-day experience. I need you to draft a 320-word press release for the listing-day ceremony of an Indian D2C nutrition brand ("FitFuel") at NSE on May 18, 2026. IPO subscribed 64 times overall, listing premium 32% over issue price of INR 410. Please provide: (1) headline capturing successful listing without violating SEBI publicity rules, (2) Mumbai-dateline lead with listing premium and oversubscription details, (3) two body paragraphs on IPO journey, allocation breakdown (QIB/NII/Retail), and IPO size INR 1,200 crore, (4) listing-day quote from MD/CEO and one investor quote, (5) factsheet table with key IPO data, (6) About-the-company boilerplate. Constraints: stay within SEBI ICDR post-issue publicity norms, avoid forward-looking statements, no promotional language about future plans, NSE branding accuracy, factor BSE simultaneous listing. Format as a publication-ready release optimised for ET, BS, Mint, Moneycontrol, BloombergQuint with embedded photo placeholders for the bell-ringing.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for EdTech Pivot Story
prompts.prompt: Act as an edtech PR specialist with 7 years of experience handling Indian edtech narratives. I need you to write a 230-word media pitch email to senior reporters at Inc42, YourStory, and ET for a Series C edtech ("Vidyut Learning") pivoting from K-12 tutoring to AI-powered upskilling for working professionals. Pivot announcement: May 13, 2026. New ARR target INR 280 crore by FY28. Please provide: (1) hook emphasising why the pivot makes sense post-Byju's reset and the GenAI moment, (2) three killer data points (current paying users 1.2 lakh, average ticket size INR 24,000, NPS 64), (3) tailored angles per outlet (Inc42: founder POV, YourStory: human story, ET: business numbers), (4) exclusive offer suggestion with risk mitigation, (5) press-kit attachments list, (6) follow-up plan over 7 days. Constraints: avoid implicit Byju's comparisons, respect investor (Lightspeed, Elevation) prior-clearance, ASCI rules for edtech advertising, sensitivity to parent-community narratives from K-12 sunset. Format as a ready-to-send pitch email with three outlet-specific variants.

prompts.title: Crisis Comms for Listed Company Whistleblower Case
prompts.prompt: Act as a corporate-governance crisis-comms partner with 16 years of experience advising NSE-listed boards. I need you to design a comms response plan for an NSE-listed mid-cap engineering company ("Bharat Engineering", market cap INR 22,000 crore) that has received a whistleblower letter alleging revenue-recognition irregularities of INR 320 crore over FY24-FY25. Audit committee has commissioned an independent forensic audit. News leaked to a financial blog on April 26, 2026. Please provide: (1) BSE/NSE intimation under SEBI LODR Reg 30 (under 100 words), (2) detailed press statement (250 words) acknowledging without admitting, (3) CEO letter to employees, (4) talking points for analyst calls, (5) 8 anticipated reporter questions with approved responses, (6) reputation-recovery roadmap for the next 90 days. Constraints: SEBI PIT Regulations on UPSI, Companies Act Section 177 on whistleblower protection, careful avoidance of language that could prejudice the forensic audit, factor proxy-advisor (IiAS, SES) likely commentary. Format as a six-section governance-grade brief with legal-review checkboxes.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Goa Founder Retreat
prompts.prompt: Act as a thought-leadership PR account manager at Edelman India with 6 years of experience handling founder events. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for an invite-only "India Founders Retreat 2026" at Taj Holiday Village, Goa, June 6-8, 2026, hosted by my client (a top-tier VC firm). 80 founder attendees, no public agenda. Please provide: (1) selective media strategy (no live coverage; embargoed roundup post-event), (2) shortlist of 6 senior journalists invited under MOU/Chatham House Rules with rationale (ET, Mint, Inc42, YourStory, FactorDaily, BS), (3) one-on-one founder-press matchmaking for 14 founders, (4) post-event package (headline announcements, founder by-lines, joint research data drop), (5) social-amplification plan respecting confidentiality, (6) sentiment risk assessment if a founder goes off-record. Constraints: confidentiality protocols, alcohol/state-rule compliance for Goa, Section 65B-style record-keeping for any quotable statements, sensitivity around portfolio-startup announcements. Format as a 3-day brief with named approvers and a confidentiality MOU template.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for Cricbuzz Podcast Appearance
prompts.prompt: Act as a sports-media coach with 8 years of experience prepping Indian cricket administrators and team owners for podcasts and long-form audio. I need you to prepare a podcast-prep brief for the CEO of an IPL franchise ("Mumbai Tigers") for a 75-minute appearance on a leading cricket podcast (Cricbuzz / The Grade Cricketer-style format). Topics: IPL 2026 mid-season review, auction strategy, and women's WPL business model. Please provide: (1) 3 narrative arcs the CEO should anchor, (2) 5 personal anecdotes (player-management moments, scouting wins) suitable for audio storytelling, (3) 12 anticipated questions including team performance, BCCI revenue share, salary cap inflation, and Hardik Pandya / Rohit-style controversies, (4) ready-made one-liners and analogies that work in audio, (5) topics to deflect (player injuries, contract specifics, board politics), (6) post-recording amplification plan with social cuts. Constraints: BCCI commercial confidentiality, IPL franchise agreement clauses, cricketer image-rights sensitivity, avoid match-fixing-adjacent topics. Format as a 3-page audio-friendly brief.

prompts.title: Press Release for ESG Sustainability Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a sustainability-PR account director at Weber Shandwick India with 8 years of experience handling ESG comms. I need you to draft a 380-word press release announcing the FY26 BRSR-aligned sustainability report of an Indian metals major ("Vajra Steel", BSE-listed, INR 1.4 lakh crore turnover). Highlights: 28% YoY reduction in Scope 1 emissions intensity, 14% renewable mix in power, 8,400 women employed in operations, INR 540 crore community investment. Please provide: (1) headline anchored to a single hero metric, (2) Mumbai-dated lead capturing BRSR Core compliance, (3) three body paragraphs covering decarbonisation roadmap, social metrics, and governance scores (rated AA by MSCI), (4) one CMD quote and one Chief Sustainability Officer quote, (5) data table with five-year ESG trends, (6) About-the-company boilerplate. Constraints: align with SEBI BRSR Core 2024, avoid greenwashing language flagged by SEBI 2024 circular, no comparisons with Tata Steel/JSW, factor analyst-day timing. Format as a ready-to-issue release with embedded ESG factsheet for ET, Mint, BS, BloombergNEF, ESG Today.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Indian Author Book Launch
prompts.prompt: Act as a literary PR specialist with 6 years of experience handling Indian non-fiction launches at Penguin Random House India and HarperCollins. I need you to write a 220-word media pitch email for a forthcoming non-fiction title "The Bharat Stack" (a deep-dive into India's digital public infrastructure - Aadhaar, UPI, ONDC, DigiLocker) authored by a former NPCI executive. Launch date: June 15, 2026, INR 699 hardcover. Please provide: (1) two subject-line variants for a tech reporter (Mint) versus a books editor (The Hindu), (2) hook positioning the book at the intersection of policy, tech, and India's Bharat-vs-India story, (3) three quotable sample lines from the book, (4) author availability for serial-extract syndication, (5) bespoke angles per outlet (op-ed, podcast, festival appearance at Jaipur LitFest 2027), (6) follow-up plan with embargo expiring June 8. Constraints: avoid policy-prescription overreach, sensitivities around Aadhaar criticism in early chapters, ensure attributable Nandan Nilekani blurb is correctly used. Format as a send-ready email plus a one-page sell-sheet.

prompts.title: Crisis Comms for Influencer Backlash Episode
prompts.prompt: Act as a digital-PR crisis specialist with 7 years of experience handling influencer-led crises in India. I need you to design a 72-hour comms response for an Indian D2C beauty brand ("BlossomCo") whose paid Instagram campaign with a celebrity influencer has triggered backlash for tone-deaf colorism (going viral on April 26, 2026 with #BlossomBoycott trending). Please provide: (1) 60-minute holding response (Instagram Story + brand X/Twitter) under 80 words, (2) 24-hour detailed apology with concrete corrective actions (campaign withdrawal, ASCI self-reporting, INR 50 lakh fund for skin-positivity creators), (3) influencer joint-statement template, (4) creator-community outreach plan to 40 mid-tier creators, (5) employee internal memo, (6) 30-day brand-rebuild content calendar. Constraints: ASCI Influencer Guidelines, IT Rules 2021, sensitivity to colorism legislation discussions in India, avoid defensive language, do not delete original posts (preserve as record). Format as a six-stage timeline with owner column and approval matrix.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Davos India Pavilion
prompts.prompt: Act as an international-PR account director with 11 years of experience handling Indian corporate presence at Davos. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for an Indian conglomerate's ("Bharat Industries") presence at WEF Davos 2027 (Jan 18-22, 2027), including hosting the India Pavilion, three CEO panel appearances, and 14 bilateral meetings. Please provide: (1) Indian and global media list (28 outlets including ET, Mint, BS, Reuters, Bloomberg, FT, WSJ, Nikkei), (2) day-wise schedule with priority panel sessions and 1:1 interview slots, (3) bilateral-meeting comms note for each (governments, customers, peers), (4) India-specific announcement calendar coordinated with Indian time-zones (peak ET/Mint deadline 6 PM IST), (5) Davos broll/photo plan plus India Pavilion social cadence, (6) post-Davos return-day Mumbai press conference plan. Constraints: WEF media rules, government delegation protocol, avoid stepping on Union Minister announcements, factor IST-CET 4.5-hour gap for embargoes. Format as a 5-day brief with co-ordination matrix across India and Davos teams.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for Pitch Magazine Feature
prompts.prompt: Act as a brand-PR coach with 9 years of experience prepping Indian CMOs for trade-press features. I need you to prepare a feature-interview brief for the CMO of a homegrown athleisure brand ("Adhira Active", INR 720 crore revenue) for a Pitch Magazine cover story on "India's Activewear Decade". Interview length: 75 minutes; turnaround time 10 days. Please provide: (1) 3 narrative pillars (brand journey, India activewear category growth, building moats vs. Decathlon/Nike), (2) 6 personal stories the CMO can deploy, (3) 10 likely questions including marketing-spend (INR 95 crore), media-mix shift to digital (now 64%), influencer strategy, and global expansion to UAE, (4) approved answers with quotable phrases for pull-quotes, (5) data points and case studies including a Big Billion Day spike, (6) photo-shoot guidance for Pitch's signature studio cover. Constraints: trade-press tone, ASCI compliance, no promotional puffery, respectful framing of competitors, factor Pitch's editorial quirks (preference for category-defining language). Format as a 4-page brief plus a quotable-quote bank.

prompts.title: Press Release for D2C Brand Acquisition
prompts.prompt: Act as a corporate-PR director with 11 years of experience handling Indian M&A announcements. I need you to draft a 360-word joint press release for the acquisition of a Bengaluru-based D2C personal-care brand ("Saundarya") by a listed FMCG major ("Bharat Consumer Products") for INR 1,840 crore (cash + earnouts). Announcement: May 4, 2026. Please provide: (1) joint headline with both companies' logos and acquirer-led framing, (2) Mumbai-dateline lead capturing strategic rationale, (3) two body paragraphs on Saundarya's INR 410 crore FY26 revenue and 38% YoY growth, plus integration plan retaining founder-CEO, (4) one quote each from acquirer MD, Saundarya founder, and lead investor (Sequoia), (5) deal-economics box (cash up-front, earnouts, lock-ins), (6) regulatory-approval timeline (CCI, NCLT). Constraints: SEBI LODR material-event compliance, CCI Section 5 thresholds, avoid revealing terms protected by NDA, no employee-headcount commentary, factor minority-shareholder optics. Format as a publication-ready release for ET, Mint, BS, Reuters, Bloomberg, with a separate analyst FAQ.

prompts.title: Media Pitch for Diwali Campaign Story
prompts.prompt: Act as a consumer-PR specialist with 6 years of experience pitching festive marketing stories to Indian press. I need you to write a 200-word media pitch email for a co-branded Diwali 2026 campaign by a leading Indian tea brand ("Chai Bharat") and a heritage sweets-maker ("Mithaas"), launching on October 8, 2026. Campaign: limited-edition gifting hampers priced INR 999-2,499, 14-city activation, INR 22 crore media investment. Please provide: (1) two subject-line variants for Campaign India and ET Brand Equity, (2) story hook centred on "India's gifting economy crossing INR 2 lakh crore", (3) three campaign data points (creative agency Ogilvy India, media AOR Madison, retail footprint 11,000 outlets), (4) collaboration angle (rare same-category-tier co-brand) for trade-press, (5) consumer-first angle for general press (Times of India weekend), (6) follow-up plan and exclusive interview offer with two CMOs. Constraints: ASCI compliance for festive claims, no health claims around sweets, avoid religious-sensitivity issues, factor Diwali-week media bandwidth crunch. Format as a ready-to-send email with two outlet variants.

prompts.title: Crisis Comms for Edtech Refund Controversy
prompts.prompt: Act as an edtech crisis-comms director with 8 years of experience advising Indian online-learning brands. I need you to design a 5-day crisis response plan for a Series D edtech ("Shiksha Plus", 8 lakh paying users) facing a viral parent-led complaint thread on Reddit India and X about delayed refunds (INR 36 crore stuck across 4,200 customers). Consumer-affairs ministry has tweeted asking for action. Please provide: (1) Day-0 holding response under 90 words, (2) Day-1 detailed statement with concrete refund commitment (full processing within 21 working days), (3) Day-2 founder video script (under 3 minutes, conversational Hindi-English), (4) Day-3 grievance-officer dedicated channel launch comms with toll-free + WhatsApp, (5) Day-4 transparency tracker (refunds processed, pending, disputed) for public dashboard, (6) Day-5 PR-led NCRM (NPS recovery) plan and ASCI disclosure update. Constraints: Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020, ASCI edtech guidelines, sensitivity to parent communities, avoid defensive language, factor potential class-action narrative. Format as a 5-day Gantt brief with daily KPIs.

prompts.title: Event Brief for Chennai Plant Inauguration
prompts.prompt: Act as a regional-PR account manager with 7 years of experience handling South-India industrial events. I need you to prepare an event coverage brief for the inauguration of a INR 4,800 crore semiconductor-assembly plant by my client (a Tamil Nadu-based conglomerate "Murugan Group") in Sriperumbudur, Chennai on June 22, 2026. Chief Guest: Hon'ble Tamil Nadu Chief Minister; Special Guest: Union Minister of Electronics & IT. Expected media: 110 journalists. Please provide: (1) bilingual run-of-show in English and Tamil with security/protocol windows, (2) media list across English business press (ET, Mint, BS), Tamil dailies (Daily Thanthi, Dinamalar), trade press (Electronics Bazaar, EFY), and broadcast (Sun News, Puthiya Thalaimurai), (3) on-ground media operations (media bus from Chennai airport, briefing room, Tamil-English interpretation), (4) Q&A anticipation matrix on India Semiconductor Mission incentives (INR 76,000 crore scheme) and TN industrial policy, (5) photo-op storyboard around CM-Minister joint moment, (6) post-event regional and national rollout. Constraints: protocol seniority, MeitY messaging alignment, factor Chennai pre-monsoon weather, avoid political comparisons. Format as a structured bilingual brief.

prompts.title: Spokesperson Prep for Republic TV Debate
prompts.prompt: Act as a high-stakes media coach with 15 years of experience prepping panelists for Indian prime-time debate shows. I need you to prepare a debate-prep brief for the spokesperson of an Indian e-commerce major ("BharatMart") for a 9 PM Republic TV debate on the new e-commerce rules under the Consumer Protection Act and alleged predatory pricing impacting kirana stores. Other panelists: CAIT representative, opposition spokesperson, anchor Arnab Goswami. Please provide: (1) 3 anchor messages crafted for shouty interruptive format, (2) 6 punchy one-liners under 12 seconds each, (3) 10 anticipated attack lines with prepared rebuttals, (4) emotional stories (kirana-on-our-platform success cases) with names and numbers, (5) techniques for handling cross-talk and forced exits, (6) digital-clip-friendly soundbites for X/Twitter virality post-show. Constraints: avoid offending CAIT's MSME constituency, no predatory-pricing admissions, comply with CCI and DPIIT FDI policy framing, sensitivity to Atmanirbhar Bharat narrative, ASCI rules. Format as a 3-page combat-ready brief with adrenaline-management notes.
